summary:
White Sponge Nevus can be associated with Neurofibromatosis Type 1 which follows an autosomal dominant inheritance pattern. This makes it inheritable via this mode based on known associations between these conditions.
The patient presents symptoms consistent with potential Neurofibromatosis Type 1; thus, any condition linked such as White Sponge Nevus would also follow similar hereditary patterns due to their shared etiology involving NF1 gene mutations passed dominantly from parent to child.
To directly address your query about identifying one specific example among options presented here:

Key Point Recap: Among listed choices related to various skin lesions/conditions, only "white spongy nevus" stands out by having strong ties back to *Neurofibromatosis* (*Nf*) where Nf1 mutation causes both disorders under discussion—making WSN’s transmission inherently tied into AD genetics too since Nf itself does so explicitly per medical literature reviewed above.
